title: lint_off not turning off warnings in some files
body:
Verilator version:

```
Verilator 5.031 devel rev v5.030-71-gd4a8cbb1d
```

Test case: [test-case.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17756938/test-case.tar.gz)

Running this command, Verilator still reports warnings in the lint_off files  
```
verilator -Wall --lint-only config.vlt EF_SRAM_1024x32_stub.v EF_SRAM_1024x32.v
```

config.vlt:
```
`verilator_config

lint_off -file "*stub*"
lint_off -file "*EF_SRAM_1024x32.v"
```

More interesting if I used `lint_off -file "*"`, all the warnings are ignored except for one. 
```
%Warning-TIMESCALEMOD: EF_SRAM_1024x32_stub.v:28:8: Timescale missing on this module as other modules have it (IEEE 1800-2023 3.14.2.3)
   28 | module EF_SRAM_1024x32_macro (DO, ScanOutCC, AD, BEN, CLKin, DI, EN, R_WB, ScanInCC, ScanInDL, ScanInDR, SM, TM, WLBI, WLOFF, vgnd, vnb, vpb, vpwra, vpwrac, vpwrm, vpwrp, vpwrpc);
      |        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                       EF_SRAM_1024x32.v:35:8: ... Location of module with timescale
   35 | module EF_SRAM_1024x32 (DO, ScanOutCC, AD, BEN, CLKin, DI, EN, R_WB, ScanInCC, ScanInDL, ScanInDR, SM, TM, WLBI, WLOFF,
      |        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                       ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/TIMESCALEMOD?v=5.031
                       ... Use "/* verilator lint_off TIMESCALEMOD */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

Possibly, wrong usage at my end. Still would like to know what would the correct usage be.
